decisions.rationale: User preference. Mature ecosystem, largest community, excellent DevTools, normalized cache. Heavier than urql (~33KB vs ~12KB) but better tooling and wider adoption.

Copy-paste component model — no runtime dependency, full ownership of code. Radix UI primitives for accessibility. CSS variable-based theming enables dark mode natively. Active community (85K+ GitHub stars).
Inherent with shadcn/ui. Tailwind dark: variant + CSS custom properties. Single class toggle on <html> element. No additional library needed.
Upload → preview first 5 rows → map columns via dropdowns → confirm and import. Guided flow reduces errors and supports non-technical users. Progressive disclosure keeps each step simple.
Resolved in plan 08. Crypto-random 8-char hex code with 7-day expiry. Owner generates code, invitee enters code to join. Transactional join with used-flag.
Unspent budget rolls into next month. Requires cumulative balance tracking: next_month_budget = base_budget + (prev_budget - prev_spent). Encourages saving and reflects real household behavior.

BudgetService.GetBudgetProgress exists in Go backend (tested) but is not exposed via GraphQL. Wire it as a new query: budgetProgress(householdID: ID!, month: String!): [BudgetProgressEntry!]! so the frontend budget list can show actual spent amounts and progress.

decisions.decision: Add client-side validation for zero/negative amounts in bill form
decisions.rationale: Backend enforces Positive() but client-side validation gives immediate UX feedback instead of a server round-trip error.

decisions.decision: Add userHouseholdIDs() membership check to all 3 report resolvers
decisions.rationale: Review blocker: SpendingByCategory, MonthlyTrend, DashboardSummary only check authentication via UserFromContext, not household membership. Any authenticated user can query any household's data. Fix: apply same pattern as budget.resolvers.go:17-31 (call userHouseholdIDs, loop to verify householdID, return access denied if not member).

decisions.decision: Add userHouseholdIDs() membership check to PreviewCSVImport resolver
decisions.rationale: Review blocker: PreviewCSVImport checks auth but not household membership. CommitCSVImport (line 78) correctly checks membership. Apply the same userHouseholdIDs() pattern for consistency.

decisions.decision: Propagate edited categories from PreviewStep back to parent wizard state
decisions.rationale: Review warning: editedCategories state in preview-step.tsx is local and never merged back to previewRows. When user changes categories in preview, the confirm step receives original suggestions. Fix: add onRowsUpdated callback to merge edited categories into previewRows before calling onContinue.

decisions.decision: Add formError state with visible feedback to add-expense and add-income forms
decisions.rationale: Review warning: handleSubmit silently returns when amount is invalid. Budget forms (bills.tsx, create.tsx) handle this correctly with formError state. Apply same pattern.

decisions.decision: Validate JWT has 3 parts before atob decoding
decisions.rationale: Review warning: auth-provider.tsx decodes JWT via atob(token.split('.')[1]) without checking parts.length === 3. Try-catch mitigates crashes but an explicit guard is cleaner.

constraints: Vite for build tooling (fast HMR, ESBuild)
Backend is Go with entgo + gqlgen (GraphQL API at /query)
CSV import must handle the existing ImportTransactions mutation
